title: JARVIS Step 45 — Offline Manual Snapshot Script + Tests
purpose: Implement the Step 44 plan (Option C): an offline, operator-run script that captures the existing read-only JARVIS status into a gitignored storage/jarvis/snapshots/ JSON file so a future, separately approved step can answer 'what changed?' with a real diff. Nothing is wired to the browser; the web surface neither imports nor invokes the script and exposes no snapshot or refresh endpoint.
relation_to_step_44: Step 44 (5d0bdf3, JARVIS_OFFLINE_SNAPSHOT_PLAN_READY) planned the offline manual snapshot mechanism and recommended Step 45 = implement the script + tests only, not wired to the web. Step 45 implements exactly that. Step 46 (ask reads latest snapshot to compute a diff) remains deferred until separate approval.
files_created_or_changed: tests/test_jarvis_snapshot_report.py (new) — 16 tests pinning write-location, content, exclusion, status-shape, and no-UI-control guarantees.
explicitly_not_done: No app.py change (the existing read-only api_jarvis_status() aggregate is imported as-is; no refactor needed).
No browser control, button, form, onclick, fetch, or scheduler.
No .gitkeep added — storage/ is already gitignored, so snapshots are never tracked and no tracked runtime file is needed.
No refresh / execution / broker / paper / live control; no data fetched; no trade run or simulated.
No trading / Factory / S26-S28 / Donchian / Hydra / storage file touched outside the gitignored storage/jarvis/snapshots/ runtime output.
script_behavior: Manual/offline only: operator runs python tools/jarvis_snapshot_report.py; the browser cannot trigger it.

Reads the existing read-only status aggregate: imports app and calls app.api_jarvis_status() (adds repo root to sys.path so direct invocation finds the module); runs no extra git/subprocess/shell, fetches nothing, calls no broker, places no trade, refreshes nothing.

Writes exactly two files, only inside storage/jarvis/snapshots/: a timestamped snapshot_<ISO-no-colons>_<microseconds>.json plus a latest_snapshot.json pointer; never stages, commits, cleans, deletes, or writes elsewhere.

Whitelist capture: only whitelisted scalar display fields are copied out of each status section; anything not whitelisted is dropped, so the snapshot is a strict subset of the already read-only status.
Fail-closed: assert_snapshot_safe() re-scans the serialized snapshot for forbidden whole-word tokens and raises if any appear; main() returns nonzero on any failure.

fields: git: branch, head, clean, dirty, modified_count, untracked_count
commander_snapshot: overall_state, headline, warnings, trading_posture, cache_status, staged_count, untracked_count
trading_detail: state, read_only(true), paper_ready(false), live_ready(false), broker_control(false), candidate_status

false_positive_handling: Word-boundary (\b) matching means legitimate compounds never trip the guard (commander_snapshot is not 'command'; an _audit report name is not 'audio'; recommended_next_actions is deliberately NOT embedded as a verbatim key list, so 'action' never appears).
tests: write_snapshot writes only the timestamped file + latest_snapshot.json, only inside the given dir, and never escapes it.
latest_snapshot.json is valid JSON and marked read_only.
snapshot includes every required read-only field (generated_at, key count/hash, git, commander, trading, cache, file_hygiene).
snapshot excludes all forbidden tokens (secret, api_key, broker_password, command, action, execute, order, trade_ticket, audio, transcript) via word-boundary check.
build_snapshot drops non-whitelisted fields (injected secret/order_ticket/api_key/command/password are stripped).
assert_snapshot_safe accepts a real snapshot and rejects a planted token.
/api/jarvis/status shape unchanged (24 keys; trading flags locked false).
template has no snapshot/refresh control; no /api/jarvis/snapshot or /api/jarvis/refresh route exists (404); app.py references neither.
All temp writes use tmp_path, so the real storage tree is never touched.

runtime_files_and_gitignore.note: storage/ is gitignored (.gitignore), so snapshot artifacts are runtime-only and never enter version control.

conclusion: JARVIS now has an offline, operator-run snapshot script that captures the existing read-only status aggregate into a gitignored storage/jarvis/snapshots/ JSON file (timestamped + latest_snapshot.json pointer), using a strict whitelist plus a fail-closed forbidden-token guard. It is not wired to the browser, adds no endpoint/UI/scheduler/refresh, performs no execution and no trading control, and writes only inside the snapshots dir. 378 tests pass (+16). A real run produced a clean 24-key snapshot that git ignores. Nothing staged or committed.
